name: CiteMe
description: An AI-assisted citation generation system for essays and research drafts. CiteMe combines a Vue 3 editor, FastAPI services, vector retrieval, web/PDF source processing, and source credibility scoring to generate in-text citations and references.
what: CiteMe is a full-stack academic writing tool. The frontend is a Vue 3 single-page app with an editor, citation-source workflows, a preview/export surface, and a sidebar for source analysis. The backend is split into two Python FastAPI services: a main citation service that searches, scrapes, chunks, embeds, reranks, and generates citations, and a separate metrics service that scores source credibility. Users can auto-discover sources, provide URLs to cite from the web, or submit direct source content for citation generation.
why: I wanted to make the citation workflow less mechanical for students writing essays and thesis-style documents. The interesting part was not just formatting references, but building a system that could retrieve supporting sources, generate citations in context, and expose some signal about source quality instead of treating all sources as equal.
impact: The project shipped as a live web app and demonstrates a stronger applied-AI workflow than a simple citation formatter: retrieval, scraping, chunking, vector search, LLM generation, and credibility scoring all sit in the user path. The public demo previously lived at citeme.blucoder.cc; the project is no longer operational.
